title: thymus serpyllum var. silvicola
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. silvicola, also known as wild thyme, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family and native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of wild thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and improve digestion. The herbal preparations of wild thyme include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
